frontend_entrypoint.description: Deliver a minimal, runnable application with core UI and localStorage interaction.

running_balance.description: Implement running balance calculation and display, updating on data entry

style_entry_form.description: Refactor the style.css code for the Entry Form based on the finalized design/design_tokens.md. Confirm that all design elements are implemented according to the spec.

entry_list.description: Implement display of transactions in the Entry List component, pulling data from `localStorage`

frontend_data_entry.description: Implement data entry form functionality with validation and error handling in `app.js`

local_storage_persistence.description: Implement localStorage data persistence with encryption

style_entry_list_balance.description: Styling the Entry List and Balance Display components

local_storage_handler.description: Handles localStorage interactions with error handling

app_error_handling.description: Implement basic error handling and display error alerts using the standardized component.

docker_infra.description: Update docker-compose.yml to include necessary environment variables (encryption key).

frontend_validation.description: Implement and integrate validation functions for the finance tracker app

test_local_storage.description: Completing unit tests for local storage

test_input_validation.description: Finalize unit tests for input validation functions

local_storage_encryption.description: Verifying and correcting AES-256 encryption and Transaction object compliance in localStorage

test_local_storage_encryption.description: Adding unit tests for local storage encryption

system_designer.text: Can you please provide the Transaction and APIResponse interfaces in types.ts? I need them to implement the createTransaction function and persist data to local storage.

Please confirm that the Transaction interface is complete and that you have included all necessary fields. Also, clarify the technology stack being used for the UI and state management, if any. Finally, can you create a types.ts file to store the Transaction and APIResponse interfaces?

Please clarify the technology stack being proposed for the UI and state management. Also, can you specify how the integration order is documented in design/architecture_spec.md? Finally, can you add a section about error handling, versioning, and security to the architecture spec?
Please define the types.ts file including the Transaction and APIResponse interfaces.
I am defining the TransactionService API and data model. Please ensure your data migration strategy is compatible with AES-256 encrypted localStorage.

api_designer.text: I have defined the Transaction and APIResponse interfaces in types.ts. Please ensure your createTransaction function uses these interfaces.

I noticed that your description seems to be for a different project (climate data dashboard instead of a personal finance tracker). Please focus on the personal finance tracker requirements. Also, can you provide the API endpoints required for the Personal Finance Tracker? Include the method, path, request/response schemas, and authentication mechanisms for each endpoint (e.g., createTransaction, getTransaction, updateTransaction, deleteTransaction, listTransactions). How will real-time updates be achieved?

Please define the createTransaction, deleteTransaction, and updateTransaction functions in detail, including request and response schemas and error handling. Refer to section 4 of the architecture_spec.md file for context.

Please implement the localStorage API functions as defined in the architecture_spec.md file. Specifically, createTransaction, readTransactions, updateTransaction, deleteTransaction, and filterTransactions. Ensure proper error handling and adherence to the APIResponse interface.
I am defining the data migration strategy. Please ensure that the new TransactionService API takes into account the existing data format from Sprint 1 to facilitate migration.

Please provide precise signatures for each TransactionService method (parameters and return types), request and response schemas, error handling details, and example payloads in the architecture_spec.md file. Also, the manager suggested removing AES-256 encryption.

Please ensure the LocalStorageService implementation in app.js uses the local_storage_handler.js for localStorage interactions. Also, ensure the encryption logic is encapsulated in a separate module (e.g., encryption.js) as mentioned in data_migration_strategy.md. The dataVersion key check should occur before attempting decryption.

I have defined the data model and data migration strategy in architecture_spec.md and data_migration_strategy.md. Please use these documents to define the TransactionService API and input validation logic.

db_designer.text: Can you please include the types.ts file in the design/ directory and link it in the architecture_spec.md? Also, can you address what happens when we want to move from local storage to a database? How is the design accounting for that?
I am defining the TransactionService API and data model. Please ensure your input validation logic aligns with the types and constraints I define.
I have updated the architecture_spec.md and data_migration_strategy.md files. Please coordinate with local_storage_handler.js to avoid duplicating effort.
Please review the Transaction model in design/architecture_spec.md. Also, note that data encryption is NOT being implemented in this sprint.

design_team.text: Please ensure that the UI components (Entry Form, Entry List, Balance Display) are designed using the Transaction interface defined in architecture_spec.md. Pay specific attention to the constraints on each field (e.g., amount must be non-negative, description has a maximum length of 255 characters).

ux_researcher.text: Hi! Could you please share the Design Token Set (colors, typography, spacing, radii, shadows, motion)? I need it to create the Entry List and Balance Display Component Specs and the screen layout wireframe.

Please review the `design/design_tokens.md` file to ensure that the design tokens are complete and accurate. Also, ensure that the Entry Form specification in `design/design_spec.md` includes error states and input types for each field.

Hey, can you make sure the error alert component is designed to work well with the input validation feedback spec? Specifically, the error alert should be visually distinct from the input field error messages but consistent in tone and severity (error-500).
Please review the user flows and information architecture and provide feedback.

visual_designer.text: Hi Visual Designer, I'm updating the Entry Form component spec in design/design_spec.md to use design tokens and add accessibility details. Could you please review it when I'm done to ensure consistency with the Entry List and Balance Display specs? Also, could you please provide the visual specifications for the loading and empty states of the Entry List?

Please ensure Entry List and Balance Display components adhere to the Design Token Set (design/design_tokens.md) and the specifications in design/design_spec.md. Focus on visual language using CSS or CSS-in-JS.

Please integrate the input validation feedback spec and the error alert component into the design system specification document, ensuring consistency with existing design tokens and principles. Pay attention to spacing, color, and typography to maintain a cohesive look and feel.

Please review the finalized design tokens (typography, spacing, radius, shadow) in design_tokens.md and provide feedback on their consistency and usability. Also, prepare to define the motion design tokens once the core visual tokens and components are in place. Consider basic transitions/animations for adding/removing entries or displaying alerts.

ui_designer.text: Let's consolidate the design_spec.md file. You focus on the Entry Form component specification, and I'll focus on user flows and information architecture. We'll then combine our work into a single design_spec.md file.

Let's consolidate design_spec.md. I'll focus on the Entry List and Balance Display components, and you can own the Entry Form. We'll make sure everything references the design tokens in design_tokens.md.

Please ensure that the EntryForm component uses the visual tokens defined in `design/design_tokens.md`. Also, ensure that the input fields have labels and that the component is accessible. Review the error states and the input types to be used for each field.

Please ensure the EntryForm component uses the Input Types, Error Handling, and Success/Failure flows defined in design/design_spec.md. Also, make sure the component uses the design tokens defined in design/design_tokens.md

Please integrate the user flow from design/user_flow.md into the design spec. Also, ensure that the input validation feedback spec includes a specific example with a screenshot, as requested by the manager.

frontend_developer.text: Please use the updated design spec and tokens.

qa_engineer.text: Please use the updated design spec and tokens to validate the implementation.

Engineering Team.text: How will the application be packaged and deployed for testing? What is the entry point?

engineering_team.text: What is the build process for the application? How will `app.js` be transpiled and made available for testing? What is the entry point for the application?

qa.text: I have added a data encryption justification. Please review this and verify this is the correct approach.
Please review the TransactionService API in design/architecture_spec.md. Also, note that data encryption is NOT being implemented in this sprint.
Please create unit tests for the data migration logic in LocalStorageService. Consider edge cases such as corrupted data in localStorage and failed decryption attempts.

Software Developer #3.text: I'm implementing input validation in app.js. Can you provide the standardized component for displaying error alerts so I can integrate with it?

Dev 1.text: Hi Dev 1, I'm writing unit tests for local storage and need to test the encryption. Could you please share the encryption and decryption functions you are using in `local_storage.js` or `app.js`? Also, what encryption library are you using? Thanks!
